commit.message:
feat: upload .engine files to Supabase Storage after TensorRT conversion

After converting ONNX → TensorRT, the .engine file is uploaded to
Supabase Storage as a device-specific backup. Storage path includes
hardware model and TensorRT version for clear identification:

  models/engines/{jetson-orin-nano}_trt{10.3}/{leaf_type}_student.engine

Device info auto-detected from /proc/device-tree/model and dpkg.
Upload is best-effort — failure doesn't block setup.

NOTE: .engine files are NOT portable across GPU architectures.
Each device must either convert ONNX locally or download an engine
built on identical hardware + TensorRT version.

commit.message:
fix(#195): switch to PendingIntent OAuth delivery for Samsung compatibility (#198)

* fix(#195): switch to PendingIntent OAuth delivery for Samsung compatibility

Replace ActivityResultLauncher-based OAuth flow with AppAuth's
performAuthorizationRequest() + PendingIntent delivery.

Samsung's aggressive memory management on S23 Ultra (Android 16) kills
AppAuth's AuthorizationManagementActivity while the Chrome Custom Tab is
open, causing the ActivityResultLauncher callback to never fire and leaving
the user stuck on the sign-in screen.

Changes:
- HuggingFaceAuthRepository: replace buildAuthIntent() with startAuthFlow()
  and add deliverAuthResponse(intent) + keep handleAuthResponse(intent)
- HuggingFaceAuthManager: implement startAuthFlow() using
  performAuthorizationRequest() with PendingIntents pointing to MainActivity;
  implement deliverAuthResponse() dispatching token exchange on IO
- AndroidManifest.xml: add android:launchMode="singleTop" to MainActivity
  so onNewIntent() is called on the existing instance instead of re-creating
- MainActivity: inject HuggingFaceAuthRepository; override onNewIntent() to
  detect AppAuth response extras and call deliverAuthResponse()
- OnboardingViewModel/Screen: replace buildAuthIntent()+handleAuthResponse()
  with startAuth(); remove ActivityResultLauncher wiring
- SettingsViewModel/Screen: same replacement as Onboarding

The isAuthenticated StateFlow is updated by deliverAuthResponse() on success,
so the Onboarding and Settings screens react automatically via their existing
Flow collection — no additional wiring needed.

Closes #195

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): authResult SharedFlow for UI feedback + Mutex for sign-out race

- Add authResult: SharedFlow<Result<Unit>> to HuggingFaceAuthRepository interface
- Implement _authResult MutableSharedFlow in HuggingFaceAuthManager; emit from deliverAuthResponse()
- Replace ad-hoc CoroutineScope() calls with class-level managerScope (SupervisorJob + IO)
- Add authMutex (Mutex) to serialise handleAuthResponse state-write and signOut
- Restructure handleAuthResponse: token exchange in suspendCancellableCoroutine stage 1,
  persist + state-update under authMutex.withLock in stage 2
- Collect authRepository.authResult in OnboardingViewModel.init → emit AuthSuccess/AuthError events
- Collect authRepository.authResult in SettingsViewModel.init → emit saveSuccess/saveError
- Fix pre-existing OnboardingScreen.kt: uiState.downloadState → uiState.gemmaDownloadState

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add per-session asyncio locks for PTY write serialization

Dashboard WebSocket resize events and MCP tool writes (write_to_pane,
run_in_pane, send_keys, write_raw_bytes) were racing on the same PTY,
producing garbled input like 'RESRe-run s2-posix...IZE:235,59'.

Add zpilot.pty_lock module with per-session asyncio.Lock registry.
All local PTY write paths now acquire the session lock before writing,
making writes atomic. Lock granularity is per-session so different
sessions remain fully concurrent.

Files changed:
- pty_lock.py: new module with get_session_lock/remove_session_lock
- ops.py: lock in write_to_pane, run_in_pane, send_keys, write_raw_bytes
- mcp_http.py: lock in /ws/pty WebSocket recv loop (resize + input)
- web/app.py: lock in /api/send, /api/keys, /api/resize, /ws/terminal,
  /ws/pty WebSocket handlers

Release notes

Sourced from pyrefly's releases.

Pyrefly v0.60.0

Full Changelog: https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly/compare/0.59.1...0.60.0

Pyrefly v0.59.1

Fixed a performance regression in 0.59.0.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly/compare/0.59.0...0.59.1

Pyrefly v0.59.0

Status : Beta
Release date: March 30, 2026

Pyrefly v0.59.0 bundles 153 commits from 20 contributors.

✨ New & Improved

Area What’s new
Type Checking - You can now use while...else statements with returns in the else clause without triggering a false positive missing-explicit-return error. - Pyrefly now correctly handles type inference for nested empty dictionaries when constructing TypedDict instances, avoiding implicit-any errors. - Error messages now highlight related code with inline labels; for example, an unsupported * operation will show the types of both operands directly in the source snippet
Language Server - LSP hover information for classes now displays constructor signature and docstring. - Support additional LSP functionality for notebooks, including find-references and rename.
Performance - Faster typechecking in large pythonc codebases, up to 2x faster on recent benchmarks on real world projects - Reduced CPU usage through smarter caching of module resolution results - Improved performance of the LSP server by reducing redundant workspace diagnostic publishes.

🐛 bug fixes

We closed 16 bug issues this release 👏

  • #2026: Fixed an issue where recursive bounded generics were incorrectly reported as object, ensuring accurate type checking.
  • #2812: Resolved a false positive invalid-type-var error when persisting the get method of a fully-annotated dict.
  • #2804: Fixed an implicit-any false positive that occurred with TypedDict items, improving code readability.
  • #2868: Pyrefly now correctly recognizes while...else statements with returns in the else clause as exhaustive.
  • #2814: Enhanced hover information for datetime.datetime imports to display constructor signatures and docstrings.
  • #2896: Fixed a bad-argument-type error that occurred when using double-underscore arguments.
  • #2893: Pyrefly now correctly handles dict Literal key types as subtypes of str key types.
  • #2865: Resolved an issue where tuple subclasses with overridden __getitem__ were not recognized.
  • #2871: Fixed a false positive error when using isinstance with type | X.
  • And more! #2444, #1270, #2900, #2862, #2853

Thank-you to all our contributors who found these bugs and reported them! Did you know this is one of the most helpful contributions you can make to an open-source project? If you find any bugs in Pyrefly we want to know about them! Please open a bug report issue here

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#196): show retry button when one model errors during parallel download

In the isDownloading branch of the download when block, add a conditional
Retry button that surfaces when uiState.anyError is true. This handles the
mixed state where Gemma-4 enters Error while FunctionGemma is still
Downloading — the ModelDownloadRow already shows the per-model error, but
there was previously no way to trigger a retry until FunctionGemma also
settled.

startDownload(GEMMA_4_E2B) is safe to call here because WorkManager's KEEP
policy leaves an already-downloading or downloaded model untouched, so only
the errored model's work chain is restarted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
